title: Lines Occasioned By The Death Of Lieutenant J ---- , Who Was Killed By A Pistol-Shot, Accidentally Discharged By His Friend, Captain B ---- .
content:
With horror dumb, tho' guiltless, stood
    Beside his dying friend,
    The hapless wretch who made the blood
    Sad from his side descend!

    "Give me thy hand; lov'd friend, adieu!"
    The gen'rous suff'rer cried!
    "I do forgive and bless thee too;"
    And, having said it, died!

    And Pity, who stood trembling near
    Knew not for which to shed,
    So claim'd by both, her saddest tear - 
    The living or the dead!
meaning:
"Lines Occasioned By The Death Of Lieutenant J ---- , Who Was Killed By A Pistol-Shot, Accidentally Discharged By His Friend, Captain B ---- ." is a quintessential example of John Carr (Sir)'s signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
